questions.question: What is the primary difference between a latch and a flip-flop?
options: A) A latch is a type of memory element, while a flip-flop is a type of logic element.
B) A latch is an asynchronous memory element, while a flip-flop is a synchronous memory element.
C) A latch is a type of digital circuit, while a flip-flop is a type of digital device.
D) A latch is a type of clocked memory element, while a flip-flop is an unclocked memory element.
questions.explanation: A latch is an asynchronous memory element, while a flip-flop is a synchronous memory element.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a storage element in sequential logic?
options: A) To perform logical operations.
B) To store data temporarily.
C) To generate a clock signal.
D) To amplify a signal.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a D-latch and a T-latch?
options: A) A D-latch is asynchronous, while a T-latch is synchronous.
B) A D-latch is synchronous, while a T-latch is asynchronous.
C) A D-latch is used for data input, while a T-latch is used for data output.
D) A D-latch is used for data output, while a T-latch is used for data input.
questions.explanation: A D-latch is asynchronous, while a T-latch is synchronous.

questions.question: What is the primary function of a flip-flop?
options: A) To generate a clock signal.
B) To store data temporarily.
C) To perform logical operations.
D) To amplify a signal.

questions.question: What is the difference between a JK flip-flop and an SR flip-flop?
options: A) A JK flip-flop is asynchronous, while an SR flip-flop is synchronous.
B) A JK flip-flop is synchronous, while an SR flip-flop is asynchronous.
C) A JK flip-flop can only be set, while an SR flip-flop can only be reset.
D) A JK flip-flop can only be reset, while an SR flip-flop can only be set.
questions.explanation: A JK flip-flop is asynchronous, while an SR flip-flop is synchronous.
